At approximately 8:33 PM a tornado began south of Kohl City, MO in Franklin County and continued northeastward for 5.5 miles before dissipating just southwest of Etlah, MO in Franklin County around 8:39 PM. Most damage was confined to tree limbs, but a few structures were damaged closer to Etlah. This tornado was assigned a rating of EF-1 with a maximum wind speed of 105 mph.

Corrects previous tornado report from 4 SSW Union. Damage surveys confirmed an EF-1 tornado stretching from Elmont, MO to the southern outskirts of Union, MO. Damage to several homes, outbuildings, telephone poles, and trees was observed. Maximum wind speeds were estimated to be 105 mph, with a path width of 100 yards. Tornado occurred between 8:29 PM and 8:51 PM.

Damage surveys confirmed an EF-1 tornado stretching from Elmont, MO to the southern outskirts of Union, MO. Damage to several homes, outbuildings, telephone poles, and trees was observed. Maximum wind speeds were estimated to be 105 mph, with a path width of 100 yards.

*** 1 INJ *** A tornado developed at 8:56 p.m. on March 14, 2025 on the east side of Villa Ridge, between American Inn Road and Saint Marys Road north of Interstate 44. The tornado very quickly intensified as it moved into a neighborhood near the intersection of Interstate 44 and US Highway 100. The tornado then moved into western St. Louis County, where it continued to produce concentrated, significant tree damage before dissipating at the bend in US Highway 100, northeast of Fox Creek.
Report of extensive damage from spotter confirmed tornado. Multiple buildings damaged and vehicles damaged on Interstate 44.
Multiple buildings suffered damage near Hwy AT and Hwy 100 near Villa Ridge.
Via email: Multiple large pine trees uprooted on property.

Via email: Large snapped tree branches and uprooted trees on property. Trees appear to have fallen on elevated deck causing it to fully collapse aside from its stairs. Large tree fell on brick chimney causing it to crumble.
Via email: Multiple healthy trees of various sizes snapped on property.

A long track tornado began at approximately 9:16 PM just north of Marquette High School in Chesterfield, MO, and continued northeast through the communities of Maryland Heights, Bridgeton, Hazelwood, Florissant, Old Jamestown, West Alton, and Alton, IL before dissipating near the intersection of Illinois 3 and Highway 67 at around 9:53 PM. The tornado caused widespread damage along its path, which grew to nearly 3/4 of a mile wide by the time it reached Old Jamestown and West Alton. The most significant damage occurred to homes in Bridgeton (122 mph) and West Alton (120 mph), and this prompted a rating of EF-2.

At approximately 9:18 PM a tornado began just south of Morse Mill, MO and continued northeastward before dissipating at the Jefferson Barracks Veterans Hospital in unincorporated St. Louis County around 9:39 PM. The most significant damage occurred in the southwest corner of Arnold, MO, where the roof of a residence was torn off on Shelley Lynn Drive. This tornado was assigned a rating of EF-2.

*** 4 INJ *** At approximately 10:20 PM a tornado began near Missouri Highway HH in the Clearwater Conservation Area to the northeast of Garwood, MO in Reynolds County and continued northeastward for nearly 30 miles before dissipating east of Jewett, MO in Madison County around 10:57 PM. The most significant damage occurred in Gads Hill and Des Arc, where a few residences were significantly damaged. This tornado was assigned a rating of EF-3 with a maximum wind speed of 155 mph.

At approximately 10:28 PM a tornado began in the northeastern outskirts of OFallon, IL and continued northeastward before dissipating near the intersection of Rose Rd and Starr Rd in far southern Madison County, IL. The most significant observed damage occurred in the Parkview Trailer Park, where multiple mobile homes were damaged or destroyed. This tornado was rated EF-1 based primarily on this damage.
Also reported quarter size hail with 1.1 inches of rain.

At approximately 10:33 PM a tornado began near Machine Shop Road due west of the community of New Baden, IL, and continued northeast before ending near Illinois 160 at approximately 10:38 PM just south of Trenton, IL. The most significant observed damage was observed along Clinton County Line Road, where the roof of a home was almost completely destroyed. This tornado was assigned a rating of EF-2.

At approximately 11:11 PM a tornado began just southwest of Hagarstown, IL in Fayette County and continued northeastward for 13.4 miles through Vandalia, IL before dissipating southwest of Sefton, IL in Fayette County around 11:23 PM. The worst damage occurred northeast of Vandalia, where several trees were uprooted. This tornado was assigned a rating of EF-1 with a maximum wind speed of 90 mph.

An EF-2 tornado with peak winds of 135 mph impacted Bollinger and Perry County in southeast Missouri and Randolph County in southwest Illinois. The most severe damage occurred on the south and east sides of Perryville, MO, where a few homes were severely damaged and a school building had the exterior walls of the top story collapsed. Hundreds of trees were damaged along the path.

Damage survey crews confirmed what was likely an EF0 tornado just north of the community of St. Libory, IL. Damage indicators were very sparse, and largely limited to a two-car garage with collapsed walls. Debris from this garage was also flung several hundred yards downstream. No damage was observed to adjacent trees and a residential home within feet of this garage, and this residence was surrounded by bare, untilled field with no damage indicators. An RV/trailer was also tipped over ~1 mile downstream, but this was more likely to have been straight-line wind.
Tree limbs and powerlines down near W Kell and N Cherry. Time estimated by radar.

A brief tornado touched down just west of Bates Creek Camp near Potosi, MO during the afternoon of April 29th, 2025. Here the tornado caused building and tree damage, most notably it destroyed the garage of a home in Bates Creek Camp. The tornado then moved northeastward into southern Potosi, causing tree damage along the way. It then lifted near Washington County Memorial Hospital where it caused minor tree damage.

The tornado touched down near Eight Wheeler Road outside of Richwoods and caused tree damage as it moved north-northeast before dissipating just north of Highway H. The peak wind speed was 80 mph (EF-0), the path was 2.2 miles long and 140 yards wide.
